name: gameplay-music
description: For game music / interactive audio scoring. Segments by gameplay phase transitions, UI events, and state changes rather than cinematic cuts.
density_rationale: Gameplay state changes happen rapidly and are not always accompanied by dramatic visual cuts. Dense sampling + OCR captures UI-driven triggers that histogram/scenedetect miss.
perspective: Identify gameplay phases and state transitions first. Every UI title, phase change, boss entrance, result screen, or HUD state shift is a potential music cue point. Instrumentation and dynamic suggestions should reference game state (e.g. 'exploration loop', 'combat layer 2', 'victory sting') not just visual mood.

segmentation.description: Segment by gameplay phase transitions and UI-driven events. The scan stage uses cut-based detection (scenedetect or histogram), which often under-segments gameplay because the 3D scene changes constantly while semantically important boundaries (UI titles, phase transitions) occupy only a small portion of the frame. This strategy guides: (1) suggest-merges to weight OCR continuity and UI overlay changes higher, (2) LLM fill-in to identify phase boundaries from keyframes, (3) LLM to use _split_at annotations when the scan missed a boundary. Always run with --ocr to capture UI text triggers.
split_triggers: Game phase transition (menu -> loading -> gameplay -> result)
UI title or overlay appearance (full-screen titles, boss names, phase labels, countdown text)
Gameplay state change (exploration -> combat, normal -> boss phase, phase 1 -> phase 2)
Cutscene start or end within gameplay
Significant HUD state change (new objective, ability unlock, health critical threshold)
merge_bias: Merge consecutive frames within the same gameplay phase if no UI state change occurs
Merge across minor camera angle changes during continuous combat
Do NOT merge across UI title appearances — these are hard music cue points
Do NOT merge across phase transitions even if the visual environment is the same
Do NOT merge across cutscene boundaries
keyframe_priority: Frame showing the UI title or overlay text (the trigger moment itself)
Frame showing the clearest game state indicator (HUD, score, phase label)
Frame showing the gameplay action at peak intensity for the phase
For transition blocks, frame at the exact moment of state change

fill_guidance: **game_phase**: the gameplay state during this block (e.g. 'menu/lobby', 'countdown', 'exploration', 'combat phase 1', 'boss encounter', 'victory', 'defeat', 'result screen', 'cutscene')
**mood**: emotional intensity AND gameplay tension (e.g. 'calm exploration', 'rising threat -> combat engagement', 'peak intensity boss phase 2', 'triumph -> wind-down')

**event**: what triggers this block boundary — focus on game state changes, NOT camera movement (e.g. 'UI title: 大战对决 appears', 'boss health drops below 50%', 'player enters arena', 'result screen with score')
**ui_text**: any on-screen text visible in the keyframe — titles, HUD labels, prompts, score displays. If OCR prefill exists, verify and clean up. If no OCR was run, transcribe from keyframe.

**music_note**: music direction tied to game state (e.g. 'transition sting at title reveal, then hard-cut to combat loop', 'layer in percussion when boss appears', 'strip to ambient pad during exploration')
**music_layer**: which layer of an adaptive music system this block uses (e.g. 'ambient base', 'combat layer 1', 'combat layer 2 + boss percussion', 'victory sting -> result loop')
**rhythm_change**: BPM and pulse changes tied to game phase (e.g. 'no pulse in menu -> 120bpm combat loop at battle start', 'accelerate to 140bpm at phase 2')
**instrumentation**: specific instruments/textures per phase (e.g. 'exploration: acoustic guitar + light pad; combat: distorted guitars + taiko + synth bass')
**dynamics**: game-state-driven dynamics (e.g. 'pp during menu -> subito ff at battle start -> mp exploration after victory')
